title: [HW] Initial commit for a `hw.generate` operation and lowering
body:
This is an initial commit for a `hw.generate` operation and associated lowering.
The `hw.generate` operation represents a hardware generation construct. The operation defines an SSACFG region wherein `scf` dialect operations can be used to describe generic hardware generation constructs based on parameters provided during `hw.instance`-iation.

An initial lowering pass supports `scf.for` operations with statically determinable bounds, described through constants or HW module parameters. Future work may support more `scf` operations such as `if, while, ...`.
An alternative lowering could be implemented to emit `hw.generate` internals as `sv.for/sv.generate` ops (as proposed by @seldridge).

An example test is provided which implements a generic module for summing an array of values.

This commit relies on #2703 which adds support for parametric arrays. Given this, the important files of this PR (until #2703 is merged) is `HWMiscOps.td, HWElaborateGenerate.cpp` and `generate.mlir`.

todo:
- [x] create `scf.for` lowering
- [ ] split module specialization and `hw.generate` elaboration into two separate passes.
